steps.title: Identify the weak point
steps.content: The underlined claim says the nine notches were probably made by one individual at one time as a way of counting something. That claim depends on treating unevenly spaced, approximately parallel notches made with a single stone tool as evidence of numerical recording.

steps.title: Look for another purpose
steps.content: A weakening finding should show that the same pattern could plausibly mean something other than counting. If unevenly spaced parallel lines also appear in decorative Neanderthal art, then the pattern on the hyena bone would not uniquely point to recorded numerical information.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content: Choice A connects parallel lines to modern humans' number systems, so it would not weaken the counting interpretation. Choice B mentions another artifact with notches but gives no purpose for those marks. Choice C discusses the difficulty of making evenly spaced lines, while this artifact has uneven spacing. Choice D gives a non-numerical use for the same kind of pattern: decoration. The answer is D.
